name: python3-sphinx
title: python3-sphinx
description:
documentation generator for Python projects
Sphinx is a tool for producing documentation for Python projects, using
reStructuredText as markup language.
.
Sphinx features:
* HTML, CHM, LaTeX output,
* Cross-referencing source code,
* Automatic indices,
* Code highlighting, using Pygments,
* Extensibility. Existing extensions:
- automatic testing of code snippets,
- including docstrings from Python modules.
.
Build-depend on sphinx if your package uses /usr/bin/sphinx-*
executables. Build-depend on python3-sphinx if your package uses
the Python API (for instance by calling python3 -m sphinx).

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use sphinx-apidoc because documentation generator for Python projects.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use sphinx-apidoc as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sphinx-apidoc [OPTIONS] -o <OUTPUT_PATH> <MODULE_PATH> [EXCLUDE_PATTERN ...]` as documented upstream for sphinx-apidoc.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair sphinx-apidoc with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `sphinx-apidoc --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: sphinx-apidoc - Sphinx API doc generator tool
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install sphinx. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `sphinx-apidoc [OPTIONS] -o <OUTPUT_PATH> <MODULE_PATH> [EXCLUDE_PATTERN ...]`. documentation generator for Python projects.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: documentation generator for Python projects.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: sphinx-apidoc - Sphinx API doc generator tool.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install sphinx
Install with: sudo apt install sphinx
